title: Bifrost: Unlocking Bitcoin DeFi on Cardano — Road to Mainnet (Phase 1 of 2)

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "By the end of Phase 1, the proposers expect Bifrost to be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in federated and SPO-threshold custody modes under controlled access." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's existing SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as a native Cardano asset." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal targets 1,200 BTC in Cardano DeFi TVL, about 600,000 annual transactions, and 3,000 fBTC-holding wallets by Q2 2029; Provide milestone-gated disbursement. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score -0.15; confidence 75.7%.
rationale.summary_raw: Advisory model lean was -0.030 (display cap ±0.05) and had zero influence on the binding score: The extracted claims show a large treasury request for security-critical bridge infrastructure with plausible public-benefit upside, but the strongest treasury-relevant points around mainnet readiness, SPO-secured custody, adoption targets, fee surplus, and independent stewardship are mostly proposer-asserted while the deterministic assessment already labels claims and evidence as thin. A small cautionary nudge is justified because evidence quality and treasury stewardship matter more than speed or projected ecosystem growth.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal targets 1,200 BTC in Cardano DeFi TVL, about 600,000 annual transactions, and 3,000 fBTC-holding wallets by Q2 2029.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund Phase 1 of 2 for Bifrost, taking a Bitcoin-Cardano bridge from working testnet to launch readiness.

Deliverables: Hardening, security audits, formal verification, ecosystem and partner readiness, and stewardship and economic foundations for launch readiness., An audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in both custody modes, federated and SPO threshold, under controlled access by the end of Phase 1., Stewardship structure, hardened economic model, and SPO/dApp partner pipeline required for public launch., Public rollout and 24 months of operations are deferred to a Phase 2 proposal in Q1 2027.
Deadline/expiry: 9-month delivery period from July 2026 to March 2027

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's existing SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as a native Cardano asset.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal states that Bifrost is currently live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14 with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposers request ₳12,332,031, approximately $1,973,125 at 0.16 USD/ADA and including a 10% refundable contingency, for a 9-month Phase 1 delivery period.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): By the end of Phase 1, the proposers expect Bifrost to be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in federated and SPO-threshold custody modes under controlled access.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal claims custody of locked BTC will be distributed across 400+ Cardano SPOs weighted by delegation, rather than held by a company, foundation, or fixed signing committee.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal claims fBTC will be issued and burned in 1:1 correspondence with BTC locked in the Bifrost custody script on Bitcoin.
missing: Independent evidence for: By the end of Phase 1, the proposers expect Bifrost to be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in federated and SPO-threshold custody modes under controlled access.

Independent evidence for: The proposal claims custody of locked BTC will be distributed across 400+ Cardano SPOs weighted by delegation, rather than held by a company, foundation, or fixed signing committee.

Independent evidence for: The proposal claims Bifrost will be public infrastructure with no bridge token, no founder allocation, Apache 2.0 open source components, public reporting, and independent stewardship to be determined in Phase 1.
Independent evidence for: The proposal targets 1,200 BTC in Cardano DeFi TVL, about 600,000 annual transactions, and 3,000 fBTC-holding wallets by Q2 2029.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's existing SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as a native Cardano asset." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "By the end of Phase 1, the proposers expect Bifrost to be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in federated and SPO-threshold custody modes under controlled access." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
